members.name: swarm-s1
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Analyze Claude Code competitor

**Task ID:** s1
**Title:** Analyze Claude Code competitor

Review the Claude Code source code located at ../claude-code-sourcemap. Analyze its architecture, key features, UI/UX patterns, capabilities, and unique differentiators. Document findings in a structured format including: core features, technical approach, strengths, and gaps. Output a JSON file to target/temp/swarmout/claude-code-analysis.json with your findings.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task.

IMPORTANT: Your VERY FIRST action must be a tool call. Call `team_read_messages` with team_name set to the team name from your context. Do NOT respond with text first — call the tool immediately.

After reading messages, do the work described above using tool calls (glob, read, bash, etc.). When done, call `team_task_complete` to mark task "s1" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

**Task ID:** s5

members.name: swarm-s6
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Synthesize analyses and generate feature recommendations

**Task ID:** s6
**Title:** Synthesize analyses and generate feature recommendations

Wait for all competitor analyses (s1, s2, s3, s4) and current product review (s5) to complete. Read all JSON analysis files from target/temp/swarmout/. Compare the current product against each competitor, identify feature gaps, and consolidate unique features that competitors have but the current product lacks. Generate a comprehensive list of recommended feature additions with rationale. Output as target/temp/swarmout/feature-recommendations.json.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task.

IMPORTANT: Your VERY FIRST action must be a tool call. Call `team_read_messages` with team_name set to the team name from your context. Do NOT respond with text first — call the tool immediately.

After reading messages, do the work described above using tool calls (glob, read, bash, etc.). When done, call `team_task_complete` to mark task "s6" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

## Swarm Task: Create implementation plan with milestones

**Task ID:** s7
**Title:** Create implementation plan with milestones

Read the feature-recommendations.json file. Prioritize recommended features based on impact, feasibility, and strategic value. Create a detailed implementation plan that includes: feature prioritization rationale, milestone breakdown (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4 or similar), specific implementation tasks for each milestone, resource requirements, and risk assessment. Organize the plan in a clear structure with dependencies between tasks. Output as target/temp/swarmout/implementation-plan.json.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task.

IMPORTANT: Your VERY FIRST action must be a tool call. Call `team_read_messages` with team_name set to the team name from your context. Do NOT respond with text first — call the tool immediately.

## Swarm Task: Generate final SWARMOUT2.md document

**Task ID:** s8
**Title:** Generate final SWARMOUT2.md document

Read all analysis files and the implementation plan. Create the final comprehensive SWARMOUT2.md document at target/temp/swarmout/SWARMOUT2.md. Structure it with: Executive Summary, Current Product Overview, Competitive Landscape Analysis (section for each competitor), Feature Gap Analysis, Recommended Features (with business rationale), Implementation Plan with Milestones and Tasks (organized by priority/timeline), Risk Assessment, and Conclusion. Use clear formatting, tables, and hierarchical structure for readability.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task.

IMPORTANT: Your VERY FIRST action must be a tool call. Call `team_read_messages` with team_name set to the team name from your context. Do NOT respond with text first — call the tool immediately.
